name: Strait of Hormuz
summary: Day 92 (Saturday May 30). **HORMUZ STILL ACTIVELY CONTESTED INSIDE THE CEASEFIRE — NEW SUSPECTED FLOATING MINE OFF OMAN, 6th BLOCKADE-VESSEL INTERDICTION, AND A TOLL DISPUTE NOW PITTING THE US AGAINST ITS OWN MEDIATOR. Oman's Maritime Security Centre warned on 30 May of a floating object suspected to be a naval mine, sighted west of the Inshore Traffic Zone within Omani territorial waters, urging vessels to keep clear (CONFIRMED — Oman MSC advisory). The US military disabled the Gambia-flagged Lian Star in the Gulf of Oman (6th vessel stopped during the blockade) after it tried to enter Iranian ports; Hegseth: the 'blockade is very much still in place,' Hormuz must remain 'an open, toll-free strait' (CONFIRMED). Separately, the US Treasury reportedly threatened to sanction OMAN if it imposes Hormuz transit tolls (REPORTED — single ToI liveblog headline) — directly tied to Iran's desired Oman-mediated toll mechanism in the draft MOU; the toll question is now a live point of friction with the mediator itself. This is fresh operational evidence Hormuz remains mined and contested even as Brent de-prices the tail (~$92, -22% MoM) — the physical floor under oil. The blockade-active reality sits AGAINST the MOU's term ('blockade lifted in proportion to restored shipping').** Day 90 of Hormuz blockade (Thursday May 28). **THE STRAIT IS STILL BEING ACTIVELY CONTESTED INSIDE THE CEASEFIRE — early May 28 US forces downed FOUR Iranian one-way attack drones near Hormuz and struck an Iranian ground-control station near Bandar Abbas the Pentagon said was 'reportedly preparing to launch a fifth' drone (CONFIRMED ACTION — CENTCOM via CNN/NBC/Euronews/WaPo). This is the cleanest operational support yet for the 'physical risk persists' thesis: Iran is still contesting the strait with drones and mines even as the (unsigned) MOU calls for Hormuz to be 'open with no tolls' and Iran to clear the mines it laid. The IRGC's intercepted ballistic missile at a US base in Kuwait was the retaliation (A1); the maritime story is the drone/mine contest, NOT a new seizure — the 'second Iranian commercial-vessel seizure within 7 days' watch EXPIRED UNTRIPPED (last confirmed seizure remains May 14 Fujairah). UKMTO has logged 41+ incidents since late February; 'reports of mines in and near the TSS persist' and GNSS interference is 'sporadic but reduced from March.' Hormuz remains effectively BLOCKED on Day 90 — no operational reopening despite the MOU's unsigned 'open with no tolls' language.** **TRIGGER DECISIONS**: the Hormuz-reopening / transit-volume triggers STAY watching (transits still minimal, no reopening); the Iran-Oman permanent-toll-mechanism trigger STAYS watching (no formal announcement). PRIOR (May 27): Day 89 of Hormuz blockade (Wednesday May 27; ~48h coverage). **THE US STRUCK IRGC FAST BOATS LAYING MINES *IN* HORMUZ (CONFIRMED ACTION, May 25) — MINES STILL BEING LAID EVEN AS THE DEAL REQUIRES IRAN TO *CLEAR* THEM. US forces struck two IRGC fast boats laying mines in the strait + a Bandar Abbas SAM site (CENTCOM 'self-defense,' 'restraint'; CNN/NBC/Al Jazeera/NPR) — the cleanest evidence that the physical risk inside Hormuz is still active even inside the ceasefire (validating Croft's 'insurers/shippers won't rush back' thesis). The deal is 'largely negotiated' (Trump): Hormuz reopened 'with no tolls' + Iran clears its mines, US lifts the blockade + sanctions waivers — but Hormuz control/status remains one of three unresolved sticking points, and Bagheri Kani said 'the two sides had not yet agreed on lifting the blockade.' Separately, the Quad Foreign Ministers (New Delhi, May 26) issued a joint statement of 'deep concern over the Iranian closure' of Hormuz, reiterated UNCLOS, and explicitly 'opposed any future measures inconsistent with UNCLOS, including imposition of tolls' — India put its name to anti-toll language (CONFIRMED diplomatic action; A6).** Operationally Hormuz transits remain minimal — no operational reopening; the strait has been effectively closed 89 days. **TRIGGER DECISIONS**: transit-volume and second-seizure triggers stay watching (transits still minimal; no Iranian seizure — the May 26-27 maritime story is the US strike on IRGC boats). Iran-Oman permanent toll mechanism trigger stays watching (Fars 'management' = pre-positioning, not a formal announcement). PRIOR (May 25): **MANAGEMENT-VS-NO-TOLLS CONTRADICTION SHARPENED + 30/60-DAY SPLIT + EU SANCTIONS FRAMEWORK NOW CONFIRMED-ADOPTED.** The Axios/US reconstruction now states the strait would be 'open with no tolls' and Iran would clear the mines it laid, the US lifting the blockade in exchange; Iranian outlets keep the opposite framing — Fars's 'Iran's management / not free passage as before the war' line stands uncorrected, and Tasnim allocates '30 days for procedures related to the Strait of Hormuz and 60 days for nuclear talks.' **The two public framings directly contradict each other** — Rubio's May 21 red line was that a tolling/selective-management mechanism makes a deal 'unfeasible' [CONFIRMED both framings on the record; CONTESTED which is in the text]. **Operationally Hormuz transits remain minimal — no operational reopening; the strait has been effectively closed 87 days.** Separately, the EU Council formally ADOPTED a Hormuz-disruption sanctions framework May 22 (travel bans / asset freezes on those disrupting Hormuz shipping) — upgraded from yesterday's single-source low-confidence flag to CONFIRMED across multiple European outlets, but **NO specific designations yet** (enabling instrument; listings later). The cross-current: the EU builds a Hormuz-disruption sanctions architecture exactly as the US-Iran deal would reopen the strait — a potential transatlantic divergence [CONFIRMED]. The negotiation itself moved to Doha May 25 (Iranian delegation incl. central-bank governor Hemmati working Hormuz + HEU + frozen assets with Qatar's PM). Iran-Oman permanent toll mechanism trigger Day 4 of 14 stays `watching` — Fars 'management' = pre-positioning, not a formal announcement. PRIOR (May 24): **FARS REBUTTAL DAY: IRAN'S SEMI-STATE IRGC-LINKED OUTLET STATED 'THE STRAIT OF HORMUZ WILL STAY UNDER IRAN'S MANAGEMENT' — VESSEL COUNTS RETURNING TO PRE-WAR LEVELS 'IN NO WAY MEANS FREE PASSAGE AS IT EXISTED BEFORE THE WAR' — DIRECTLY CONTRADICTS TRUMP'S 'STRAIT OF HORMUZ WILL BE OPENED' FRAMING. THE LEAKED 60-DAY MOU TEXT INCLUDES HORMUZ REOPENED TO PRE-WAR VESSEL COUNTS WITH 'RELIEF FOR PERFORMANCE' STRUCTURE (IRAN CLEARS MINES, US LIFTS BLOCKADE, IRAN SELLS OIL FREELY). PEZESHKIAN FORMULATION ('EFFECTIVE AND PROFESSIONAL MONITORING AND CONTROL MECHANISMS') LANDS CLOSER TO FARS THAN TO TRUMP. IRAN-OMAN PERMANENT TOLL MECHANISM TRIGGER DAY 3 OF 14 — RUBIO'S MAY 21 RED LINE BREACH TEST APPROACHING. AIS DATA: ~5-6 TRANSITS/DAY VS 138 BASELINE (NO OPERATIONAL REOPENING). VLCC VOLUMES -36% VS PREWAR (Lloyd's List May 22).** (1) **Fars News May 24 (CONFIRMED — Times of Israel May 23 liveblog primary, The Hill aggregator)**: 'Although Iran has agreed to allow the number of passing vessels to return to pre-war levels, this in no way means free passage as it existed before the war.' Iran's primary public position. (2) **60-day MOU architecture (REPORTED — Axios Ravid primary, FT, Al Jazeera, TOI, Xinhua)**: Hormuz reopened to pre-war vessel counts; Iran clears mines it deployed; US lifts naval blockade; Iran able to sell oil freely; 'relief for performance' principle — faster mine clearance ↔ faster blockade lift. (3) **Rubio May 24 in New Delhi (CONFIRMED — US News, CNBC)**: 'progress had been made in the past 48 hours on an outline that could help resolve the situation around the Strait of Hormuz' — first public US-side acknowledgement of forward motion. (4) **Lloyd's List May 22 'Hormuz crisis slashes VLCC volumes by 36%'**: global crude exports on VLCCs averaged 14.4m bpd over past eight weeks (down 8.1m bpd from prewar). VLCC rates stabilised around $100,000/day (vs $200K+ paper-physical divergence terminal threshold). Frontline Q1 record TCE $103,500/day, Q2 booked 82% at $181,700/day. (5) **AWRP ~1% holding** from May 19 levels (8x pre-war) — insurers continue to refuse the deal-anticipation tape; physical premium intact through Sunday. (6) **Iran-Oman permanent toll mechanism trigger Day 3 of 14 (by 2026-06-05)**: Fars's 'manage the waterway' language with vessel-count return but not 'free passage' is the public verbalisation of exactly the selective-toll mechanism Rubio's May 21 red line rejected. (7) **Helima Croft framing dominant**: 'The fine print of the agreement will matter. If Iran continues to have the final say on passage, some insurers and shippers may be reluctant to rush back in.' **The single most architecturally consequential dispute in the deal text**: BOTH sides currently believe their version is in the leak text — this is either ambiguity by design or a structural mismatch waiting to collapse. **Markets pricing deliberate-ambiguity resolution** (Brent $103.54 -5%+ weekly); **insurance market refusing the deal narrative** (AWRP unchanged). PRIOR (May 23): IRGC NAVY MAY 23 CLAIM: 25 SHIPS TRANSITED HORMUZ IN PAST 24H 'WITH IRGC COORDINATION AND SECURITY' — SECOND CONSECUTIVE DAILY CLAIM IN 25-35 VESSEL RANGE; CONTRADICTED BY BLOOMBERG/LLOYD'S/UKMTO 'LARGELY FROZEN' FRAMING. AWRP ~1% HOLDING INCONSISTENT WITH 25 DAILY SAFE TRANSITS. THE CLAIM IS PROPAGANDA LOCK-IN FOR IRAN-OMAN 'PERMANENT TOLL MECHANISM' ARCHITECTURE RUBIO'S MAY 21 RED LINE REJECTED.** Brent $103.54 May 22 close (+0.9% 1D, -1.5% 1M, +44.8% vs $73 baseline) — flat into weekend despite Hormuz Day 85 closed + IRGC daily 25-35 vessel claims. **The structural read**: market explicitly NOT pricing the May 24 strike-window; Pakistan-mediated draft text (May 21-22 leak) includes Persian Gulf and Strait of Hormuz freedom-of-navigation guarantees but does NOT explicitly address Iran-Oman 'permanent toll mechanism' parallel discussions (per Wikipedia 2026 Hormuz crisis). Two parallel tracks: (i) leaked text's freedom-of-navigation guarantee — US-acceptable; (ii) Iran-Oman toll mechanism — Rubio publicly unacceptable. **Frontline Q1 results May 22**: Atlantic-to-Pacific share of VLCC volumes now 35% (vs 22% pre-war baseline); Q2 contracted at $181,700/day on 82% of VLCC days = physical book locked. Lloyd's List May 22: 'Hormuz crisis slashes VLCC volumes by 36% but voyages are longer.' **AWRP ~1% holding from May 19 levels (8x pre-war)** — insurers have NOT bought the deal-anticipation tape; physical premium intact. **Structural premium $30+ above $73 baseline INTACT** even after tail-risk de-pricing $7-8. **Trigger**: 'Iran-Oman permanent Hormuz toll mechanism publicly announced within 14 days (by 2026-06-05)' — Rubio red line breach test. **The Pakistan-mediated text creates architecture for Hormuz partial reopening within 1-2 months IF formally announced and implemented; Iran-Oman toll-mechanism parallel track creates separate US-acceptability failure mode.** PRIOR (May 22): **CROFT 'TUESDAY CLOSE VS $112' BINARY RESOLVES TO FALSE-POSITIVE SUSTAIN-RULE FIRE — BRENT $104.52 / WTI $99.50 / STANCHART $130-145 PATH MOMENTUM EXTINGUISHED. PAKISTAN-MEDIATED DRAFT TEXT LEAKED VIA AL ARABIYA MAY 21 INCLUDES PERSIAN GULF AND STRAIT OF HORMUZ FREEDOM-OF-NAVIGATION GUARANTEES. RUBIO MAY 21 RED LINE: DEAL 'UNFEASIBLE' IF IRAN CONTINUES PURSUING TOLLING SYSTEM.** **(1) Brent crash + WTI sub-$100**: Brent settled $104.52 May 22 (-5.3% cumulative window), WTI broke $100 to $99.50 (Energy Intelligence, Fortune, CNBC); May 21-22 closes mathematically falsify May 18-19 2-session sustain rule. **(2) Pakistan-mediated draft text leak (REPORTED — Al Arabiya May 22 primary)**: Persian Gulf and Strait of Hormuz freedom-of-navigation guarantees + joint monitoring mechanism + sanctions lifted in phases on Iranian compliance; NO uranium dismantlement. **(3) Rubio May 21 red line (CONFIRMED RHETORIC — CBS News liveblog primary)**: any deal 'unfeasible' if Iran continues pursuing the implementation of a tolling system in the waterway. **The leaked Al Arabiya text addresses Hormuz freedom of navigation but does NOT explicitly address Iran's parallel Iran-Oman discussions over a 'permanent toll mechanism'** (per Wikipedia 2026 Strait of Hormuz crisis). **Two parallel tracks now exist on the Hormuz architecture**: (i) leaked deal text's freedom-of-navigation guarantee — US-acceptable; (ii) Iran-Oman permanent toll mechanism — Rubio publicly stated US-unacceptable. Deal architecture's executability partially conditional on which Hormuz framework prevails in implementation. **(4) IRGC Hormuz transit claim May 22 (CLAIMED)**: 35 vessels passed in 24 hours (IRGC primary via Iran International). Independent maritime trackers (hormuzstraitmonitor.com, IranWarLive, Bloomberg Hormuz Tracker May 17) continue 'largely frozen' framing — 35-vessels claim likely refers to ALL Hormuz transits including Iranian coastal/cabotage, NOT international VLCC/tanker traffic. **1,550 stranded vessels and 22,500 trapped mariners headline figure remains operative.** **(5) Frontline Q1 results May 22**: Atlantic-to-Pacific share of VLCC volumes now 35% (vs 22% Jan-Feb 2026 baseline) — ton-miles dislocation operationally validated at fundamental scale. Lloyd's List May 22 'Hormuz crisis slashes VLCC volumes by 36% but voyages are longer.' **Structural premium at $30+ above $73 baseline INTACT** even after tail-risk de-pricing $7-8. **NEW TRIGGER (added May 22)**: 'Iran-Oman permanent Hormuz toll mechanism publicly announced within 14 days by 2026-06-05' — Rubio red line breach test. **AWRP ~1% holding from May 19 levels** (8x pre-war) — insurers have NOT bought the deal-anticipation tape; physical premium intact. **The Pakistan-mediated text creates the architecture for Hormuz partial reopening within 1-2 months IF formally announced and implemented; but the Iran-Oman toll-mechanism parallel track creates the separate US-acceptability failure mode.** PRIOR (May 19 — Monday): **STANCHART $130-145 PATH ACTIVATES — Brent 2-session sustain rule met Monday with $111.47 +2.0% on Friday $109.26 close**; cascade to inr-usd (₹96 fired), nifty-50 (-1.04%), sp-500, marine-war-risk-insurance live. **HOUTHI RED SEA DAY 7 NEGATIVE-SPACE WINDOW CLOSES UNTRIPPED** — May 11 Hodeidah unclaimed incident reverts to 'single unclaimed event,' not start of pattern; no second incident through Monday morning IST per UKMTO. **NEW WEEKEND KINETIC TAPE**: (i) UAE MoD May 17 — 3 drones entered UAE from western border, 2 intercepted, 1 caused fire at electrical generator outside inner perimeter of Barakah Nuclear Energy Plant (IAEA 'grave concern,' radiation normal); (ii) Saudi MoD May 17 — 3 drones from Iraqi airspace intercepted over Saudi airspace, ministry 'reserves right to respond at the appropriate time and place'; UAE MoFA formal condemnation May 18 = first confirmed Saudi territorial breach of cycle. **KHARG ISLAND OPERATIONAL FOCUS PERSISTS — IRAN AIR-DEFENSE REINFORCEMENT REPORTED (NYT, single-source US intel chain) IN ANTICIPATION OF POSSIBLE US GROUND OPERATION TO SEIZE THE ISLAND.** Per NYT (Friday May 15 / Saturday May 16) and Gulf News May 17 ('Operation Epic Fury 2.0'), Iran has 'laid traps and moved additional military personnel and air defenses to Kharg Island in recent weeks' in preparation for possible US operation to take control of the island. Kharg Island is **20 miles off the Iranian mainland** in the Persian Gulf and handles **up to 90% of Iran's oil exports**. The Iranian preparation is consistent with two prior Bloomberg satellite snapshots of empty Kharg jetties (May 6-8, May 11-12) and with the broader Iranian export-collapse picture. **This is the cleanest operational confirmation in the cycle that Iran ALSO is preparing for kinetic escalation, not just rhetorical hardening.** Single-source chain (NYT citing US intel) limits verification to REPORTED — but the operational signature can be cross-checked in coming days. **A7 NO FRESH UKMTO ADVISORY through May 17 morning IST**; no second commercial-vessel seizure near Fujairah or in Qatari EEZ since May 14 Hui Chuan + Haj Ali incidents. Aramco CEO Nasser ~30 vessels/day vs 140 pre-war (partial-explanation-by-Iran-permitted-Chinese-transit) remains operative. **A8 IRAN-PERMITTED SELECTIVE CHINESE TRANSIT — no new vessel-count update over the weekend** on the '30+ Chinese ships under Iranian management protocols' tally. The trigger ('Iran-permitted Chinese transit exceeds 50 vessels/week sustained 2 weeks') is at Day 3 of the 14-day measurement window. **A6 VLCC TD3C Baltic Exchange Week 18 partial WS408.13 (~9x pre-war) carries forward as the most recent rate print; Week 19 data not yet released through May 17 morning. AWRP ~1% H&M / 7-day (~8x pre-war 0.10-0.15%) carries forward.** **The Brent 2-session-sustain test gate is Monday May 18's close**; until then, the trigger stays `watching` and StanChart $130-145 path stays `dormant`. **Markets closed Sunday — first repricing event is Monday May 18 Asian open.** **B4 HOUTHIS NEGATIVE-SPACE DAY 6 OF 7** since May 11 Hodeidah — Houthi quiet during US kinetic-preparation phase is structurally significant intelligence, not coincidence. **PRIOR (May 16):** A8 SELECTIVE REOPENING — IRAN ALLOWED 30+ CHINESE SHIPS TO TRANSIT SINCE MAY 13 NIGHT under 'Iranian management protocols'** (REPORTED — Fars/IRGC-Navy senior official primary; Iran state TV 'more than 30 ships'; Xinhua 'Iran begins letting some Chinese ships cross Strait of Hormuz' Chinese state amplification; Al Jazeera 'Trump, Xi discuss Strait of Hormuz as Chinese vessels transit key waterway'; CBS; Defense Post; Newsweek; Militarnyi; New Arab). Defense Post and Newsweek noted Iran has occasionally allowed Chinese ships even during the broader blockade because China is the largest buyer of Iranian oil. **The Aramco CEO Nasser benchmark ~30 vessels/day vs 140 pre-war is now PARTIALLY EXPLAINED**: a portion of the ~30/day is Iran-permitted Chinese transit, not non-Iranian commercial flow. **Non-Iranian non-Chinese transit rate is therefore likely *LOWER* than the headline 30/day figure implies.** This is the operational confirmation that Iran is using selective transit as a diplomatic instrument rather than enforcing a comprehensive blockade — consistent with the 'selective enforcement under PGSA rules' framing the node already carried. The simultaneity with the Trump-Xi summit is the signal: Xi's joint-statement line that 'Hormuz must remain open' coexists with operational Chinese transit dependent on Iranian management protocols. **A7 NEGATIVE-SPACE HOLDS — no fresh UKMTO advisory through May 16 morning IST** on Hui Chuan or Haj Ali; Iran has not commented on Hui Chuan; no attribution on Haj Ali; India MEA 'unacceptable' carries forward. No second commercial-vessel seizure near Fujairah or in Qatari EEZ since May 14-15. **A11 BRENT RETRACED TO ~$106 ON FRIDAY; WTI BOOKED +11% WEEKLY GAIN to near $106 — cleanest week of physical-tightness reflection in cycle**, with Hormuz blockade explicitly cited as driver. EIA confirmed Hormuz crude/fuel flows dropped by ~4 mbpd in March-April. **Baltic Exchange Week 18 partial print is the most recent (WS408.13, ~9x pre-war); Week 19 data not yet available through May 16 morning.** Paper-physical bifurcation remains structurally intact: paper-WTI weekly +11% reflects physical tightness, while US equities sold off Friday on the same data interpreted through inflation/yields. The Brent $108-sustained trigger (StanChart $130-145 cascade) is now **LESS** close to firing than yesterday's intraday $107.42 print suggested — Brent retraced. **AWRP unchanged ~1% H&M / 7-day = ~8x pre-war 0.10-0.15% baseline** (Lloyd's List May coverage; LMA: 88% of underwriters retain Gulf appetite; safety, not availability, halts transit; JMIC May 4-5: Hormuz Critical, 6 transits May 3 / 5 transits May 4 vs 138 historical). **Container freight FBX13 Asia-NEur ~$2,850/FEU (+10% week), Shanghai-Jebel Ali $8,000+ (4x pre-war) per Freightos May 12** — Hormuz is a regional, not systemic, container shock per Freightos. **Mokhber 'atomic-bomb' doctrine UNCHANGED but Xi rhetorically attacked the Hormuz toll-booth premise on May 14-15.** Trump immediately reversed post-summit and on May 16 sent Iran a new nuclear ultimatum letter — joint-statement Hormuz language now exists alongside Trump's escalatory rhetoric and the operational Iran-Chinese-selective-transit pattern. PRIOR (May 15): **TRUMP-XI JOINT STATEMENT NAMES HORMUZ DIRECTLY: 'MUST REMAIN OPEN'** (CONFIRMED — ToI May 15 liveblog primary, China MFA readout). Xi additionally opposed 'the militarization of the Strait' and any effort to 'charge a toll' for its use (a direct rhetorical strike at Mokhber's 'atomic-bomb' doctrine), and said China is interested in purchasing more American oil to reduce dependence on the Strait. **THE STRAIT REMAINS PHYSICALLY CLOSED** despite the joint statement: ~30 vessels/day vs ~140 pre-war (Aramco CEO Nasser line unchanged), Iran is proactively curbing output because storage tanks are filling, and EIA confirmed Hormuz oil flows fell nearly 30% in Q1 2026. **Diplomatic words don't reopen shipping lanes that IRGC mines and small-boat attacks are physically closing.** Trump-Xi joint statement is the third repetition of 'Hormuz must remain open' since February — strait has remained largely blocked. **TWO NEW KINETIC INCIDENTS ON SUMMIT DAY**: **B1 Honduras-flagged HUI CHUAN seized off Fujairah by 'unauthorised personnel,' reportedly bound for Iranian territorial waters** (CONFIRMED on seizure event — UKMTO primary advisory; REPORTED on Iranian destination — UKMTO investigating, no claim of responsibility). Vessel identified by BBC; Fox News reports possible 'floating armory.' Cycle's second confirmed Iranian-direction seizure on a vessel previously at anchor in UAE waters. Iran has not claimed responsibility — consistent with prior pattern of operational ambiguity. The simultaneity with the joint statement IS the signal. **B2 Indian-flagged dhow MSV HAJ ALI sunk off Oman in suspected drone attack** (CONFIRMED sinking — Bloomberg, Business Standard, MEA primary; CLAIMED on drone attribution — no confirmed attribution). 14 crew rescued by Omani Coast Guard. India MEA called the attack 'unacceptable' — strongest public Indian language on a Gulf incident in the cycle. **A10 VLCC TD3C TANKER RATE NOW CONFIRMED via Baltic Exchange Week 17 report at WS459.28 / TCE $467,408/day** — Lloyd's List $420-462K range earlier this week is now corroborated; Week 4 (late January) was WS126.5/TCE $112,394/day, so today's rate is ~4.2x late-January and ~12-15x pre-war ~$30-40K range. Frontline (FRO) tape selling on de-escalation conviction while spot rate is at multi-year highs — paper-physical divergence at cycle extreme. **A11 HORMUZ/RED SEA NEGATIVE SPACE HOLDS**: No fresh UKMTO advisory; Project Freedom Day 10 paused; CENTCOM redirect tally unchanged at 61; Qatari-EEZ Safesea Neha strike now Day 5 of 7 with no claim of responsibility and no Qatar MoFA statement. **EIA MAY STEO assumption: Hormuz effectively closed until late May, shipping picks up June; Iraq+Saudi+Kuwait+UAE+Qatar+Bahrain collectively shut in 10.5 MMbpd in April; Brent $89 4Q26.** AWRP unchanged ~1% H&M / 7-day = ~8x pre-war 0.10-0.15% baseline. Mokhber 'atomic-bomb' doctrine UNCHANGED but now diplomatically isolated — Xi rhetorically attacked the Hormuz toll-booth premise. **A8 NEGATIVE-SPACE (carrying forward): Day 4 of 7 since May 11 Hodeidah unclaimed (no 2nd Red Sea incident); Day 5 of 7 since May 10 Safesea Neha Qatari EEZ strike (no 2nd strike); Project Freedom pause Day 10; CENTCOM redirect tally unchanged at 61; no 5th disabled Iranian-flagged tanker. UKMTO 'Recent Incidents' page does not log a second incident in southern Red Sea / Hodeidah corridor through May 13-14 morning.** **A9 BLOOMBERG MAY 13 SATELLITE: KHARG ISLAND OIL JETTIES EMPTY AGAIN** = second satellite snapshot of empty jetties this cycle, operational confirmation of Iranian export collapse without kinetic premium. The National (Abu Dhabi) ran sober analytical piece May 13 explicitly NOT attributing slick to Iran ('where did it come from?') — Gulf-press editorial caution maintained. **EIA SEPARATELY CONFIRMED (Bloomberg May 13): Hormuz oil flows fell nearly 30% in Q1 2026** = official-data confirmation of Lloyd's List operational tracker series. **B3 EIA MAY STEO (CONFIRMED PRIMARY): assumption Hormuz effectively closed until late May, shipping picks up June; Iraq+Saudi+Kuwait+UAE+Qatar+Bahrain collectively shut in 10.5 MMbpd in April; Brent $89 4Q26.** Aramco CEO Nasser May 11 line carries forward: 2-5 ships/day Hormuz (vs ~6/day May 10 / ~70 pre-war), 100M bbl/week lost, 880M cumulative, market won't normalize until 2027 if disruption persists past mid-June. **VLCC TD3C ~$420-462K/day per Lloyd's List May 11 'in unchartered territory'** (vs graph's last-recorded ~$100K) = MAJOR escalation signal — paper-Brent at $105.68 vs spot VLCC at $420K is the cleanest paper-physical divergence indicator we have. AWRP ~1% H&M / 7-day = 8x pre-war 0.1-0.15%; LMA confirms market available (88% appetite hull, 90% cargo); some Hormuz crossings paying up to 10% in mid-March extreme cases. **FBX13 (China/East Asia → Mediterranean) at $4,179/FEU week ending May 12 (-5% w/w) carries forward; container freight tanker-container divergence persists.** Mokhber 'atomic-bomb' Hormuz doctrine UNCHANGED. PRIOR (May 13): **A8 NEGATIVE-SPACE INTELLIGENCE — NO 2ND RED SEA INCIDENT in 7-day window from May 11 Hodeidah (Day 2 of 7); single unclaimed remains; NO 2ND STRIKE inside Qatari EEZ following May 10 Safesea Neha (Day 3 of 7); Project Freedom pause holds Day 8 (since May 5-6); CENTCOM redirect tally unchanged at 61; NO 5TH DISABLED TANKER. UKMTO 'Recent Incidents' page does not log a second incident in southern Red Sea / Hodeidah corridor through May 12-13 morning. The non-event IS the intelligence: 75 days into Hormuz blockade and 8 days into Project Freedom pause, the kinetic ceiling held through diplomatic-rejection shock — Iran did NOT use the opening to escalate maritime pressure on either Qatari coastline or Bab el-Mandeb, which would have been the predictable retaliation playbook.** **A9 KHARG SLICK CONSOLIDATED VIA COPERNICUS: Al Jazeera + ynet + Jerusalem Post + Conflict and Environment Observatory now amplify Sentinel-1/-2/-3 imagery showing ~45 sq km west of Kharg, May 6-8 detection — the largest since the war began 70 days ago. Iranian VP Shina Ansari publicly denied Iranian-facility origin: 'foreign tanker dumping ballast water.' Fox May 11-12 ~12-20 sq km second slick is consistent with drift south rather than separate origin event — re-appearance trigger AMBIGUOUSLY ACTIVATED (drift ≠ re-appearance, ballast counter-claim not formally falsified). Western wires in slow-amplification mode on attribution.** **B3 ARAMCO STRATEGIC NARRATIVE CONFIRMED — Aramco Q1 +25% YoY off East-West pipeline reroute (Yanbu exports 770K bpd Jan-Feb → ~4 MMbpd late March → 3.66-4 MMbpd through mid-May; East-West pipeline pushed to 7 MMbpd capacity); Aramco + ADNOC reportedly 'quietly resumed limited Hormuz crude exports' (World Oil May 8). Saudi-Emirati supply ~90% preserved through non-Hormuz routing + quiet limited Hormuz transits, even as headline non-Iranian Hormuz throughput shows -96% (EIA: avg ~500,000 bpd non-Iranian crude since early March vs ~13.6 MMbpd pre-war). Explains why Brent has not gone parabolic — Gulf-state pipeline optionality is the saving grace.** **A13 KOREA HMM NAMU MAY 4 INCIDENT: South Korean government formally CONFIRMED EXTERNAL STRIKE — investigation found TWO unidentified airborne objects struck stern at ~1 min intervals ('double-tap' drone pattern); mine + torpedo formally ruled out. FM Cho Hyun May 13: 'too early to determine who was responsible.' Opposition PPP publicly criticised government for omitting Iran. Seoul institutionally maintaining 'various actors' hedge.** Aramco CEO Nasser May 11 line ('2-5 ships/day, market won't normalise until 2027 if Hormuz disruption persists past mid-June') carries forward. AWRP unchanged ~1% H&M / 7-day (8x pre-war 0.1-0.15% baseline); trigger 'AWRP normalisation toward 0.5% by May 16' Day 7 of 10 — FAILING HARDER. VLCC TD3C ~$100K/day; Frontline +1.0% 1D / +10.6% 1M / +28.6% 3M (insurers + tanker market have NOT bought de-escalation).
